name: Tadalafil did not improve 6-minute walk distance in COPD-associated pulmonary hypertension
statement: In older male veterans with COPD-associated pulmonary hypertension, tadalafil 40 mg/day did not improve 6-minute walk distance, mean pulmonary artery pressure, or pulmonary vascular resistance versus placebo, although dyspnea questionnaire scores improved at 6 months.
time_horizon: 12 months for 6MWD primary endpoint; 6 months for hemodynamics and dyspnea secondary endpoints.
quantitative_note: The trial randomized 42 subjects, all male and 68 +/- 7.6 years old; no significant difference was observed for primary 6MWD change or mPAP/PVR change, with clinically meaningful UCSD shortness-of-breath score improvement.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports design, sample imbalance, endpoints, null 6MWD/hemodynamic findings, dyspnea signal, safety, and limitations.

population_profile: Older male veterans with COPD-PH; low directness to healthy men age 30 to 50.
endpoint_measure: 6-minute walk distance, pulmonary vascular resistance, mean pulmonary artery pressure, UCSD shortness-of-breath questionnaire, major adverse events.
effect_estimate: No significant 6MWD or hemodynamic benefit; dyspnea questionnaire improved at 6 months.
safety_findings: No significant difference in major adverse events; tadalafil was well tolerated overall in the abstract.
directness_to_target_population: Low for healthy users because of older COPD-PH population and high-dose disease treatment.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Under-enrollment, group imbalance, disease-specific endpoints, and limited generalizability.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence, but disease context prevents transfer to healthy exercise capacity.
